title: [January 29, 2026] Proposed Changes
post1Raw:
## Summary

Risk Mitigation

1. [Gnosis] SparkLend - Deprecate Market Phase 1
2. [Ethereum] SparkLend - Deprecate tBTC Phase 1
3. [Ethereum] SparkLend - Deprecate ezETH Phase 1
4. [Ethereum] SparkLend - Deprecate rsETH Phase 1

Centralized Account Onboardings

~~5. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard with Paxos~~

Product Updates

6. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Upgrade ALM Controller to v1.9.0
7. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard Uniswap v4 PYUSD/USDS Pool
8. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard Uniswap v4 USDT/USDS Pool
9. [Ethereum] Spark Savings - Increase spUSDT Supply Cap

Treasury Operations

10. [Ethereum] SparkLend - Claim Accumulated Reserves and Send to Liquidation Multisig
11. [Ethereum] Spark Treasury - Spark Foundation Grant for February 2026 (Exec)

## Rationale

### [Gnosis] SparkLend - Deprecate Market Phase 1

The Gnosis Chain instance of SparkLend has relatively low usage and earns only a very small amount of reserve factor revenue for Spark. We propose to begin the deprecation process for this market to reduce Spark’s overall risk surface and eliminate a potential source of reputational risk which may occur if the market experiences a tail risk event. Spark will consider opportunities to deploy on or collaborate with the Gnosis Chain ecosystem in the future when conditions are favorable.

Parameter summary:

* Freeze reserve (all assets)
* Increase reserve factor (all assets): 50%

### [Ethereum] SparkLend - Deprecate tBTC Phase 1

tBTC has been listed for nearly a year, and during this time it has never generated a meaningful amount of stablecoin borrowing demand or protocol revenue for Spark. Retaining tBTC as a collateral asset exposes Spark to various tail risk factors, and presents an unfavorable risk/reward ratio given the low usage and revenue generating capacity. For this reason, we propose to begin the deprecation process for tBTC with the goal of reducing SparkLend’s overall risk surface and exposure to underperforming collateral assets.

Proposed actions will prevent new supply and borrow transactions and capture substantially all borrow revenue for the Spark treasury (to encourage withdrawal of tBTC supply). Future proposals may consider additional parameter changes such as reducing LTV or liquidation threshold or updating rate model, but for the time being there is no impact on existing positions using tBTC as collateral (other than inability to add additional tBTC collateral to a position).

Parameter summary:

* tBTC
  * Freeze reserve
  * Increase reserve factor to 99%

### [Ethereum] SparkLend - Deprecate ezETH Phase 1

ezETH has seen relatively limited usage since launch, currently driving just $14 million of debt collateralization which optimistically generates around $500,000 of annualized net revenue to Spark per year. The vast majority of supply, nearly 95% in the case of ezETH, is driven by a single wallet which also happens to be the majority supplier of rsETH. We see this account uses several other collateral assets from time to time including close substitutes of wstETH and weETH, so we do not expect deprecation of ezETH to result in this user leaving or reducing borrow activity significantly.

Removing lesser used collateral assets like ezETH reduces SparkLend’s overall risk surface, and may have a positive impact on acquisition and retention among risk averse users who want to minimize their tail risk.

This initial proposal will freeze the ezETH reserve, which will prevent any new supply of this asset (borrows are already disabled). Existing positions will not be impacted, and additional parameter changes including reducing LTV to 0% or reducing liquidation threshold will be considered in the future.

Parameter summary:

* ezETH
  * Freeze reserve

### [Ethereum] SparkLend - Deprecate rsETH Phase 1

Similar to above, rsETH drives a low amount of revenue generation and usage for Spark, and almost all usage is driven by a single user who has proven willing to use substitute collateral assets that will remain supported. Removing rsETH supply will improve SparkLend’s safety profile, improving risk adjusted return for Spark as an allocator and potentially helping with retention among risk averse users.

This proposal will prevent new rsETH supply (borrowing is already disabled) with no impact on existing positions at this time, and additional changes may be considered in the future.

Parameter summary:

* rsETH
  * Freeze reserve

**Edit (21 January 2026):** Unfortunately, generation of deposit addresses was not complete in time to get this item into this spell. We plan to include this item in the next available executive vote once the deposit address generation and account configuration is fully completed.

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Upgrade ALM Controller to v1.9.0

We propose to upgrade the Ethereum ALM Controller to version 1.9.0. This upgrade adds functionality to be able to integrate Uniswap v4 pools for liquidity provision (including single tick ranges that serve as limit orders) and swaps. We plan to upgrade the foreign chain controllers to the new version in a future spell.

Parameter Summary:

* Upgrade Ethereum ALM Controller to Version 1.9.0

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard Uniswap v4 PYUSD/USDS Pool

Uniswap v4 offers greater control liquidity vs alternative DEXes, allowing for placing liquidity into configurable ranges rather than having liquidity distributed across a single protocol defined curve. This opens up new capabilities for SLL, including placing single sided liquidity, single tick liquidity that effectively serve as onchain limit orders, and narrow ranges that optimize for capital efficiency.

We plan to continue to use Curve as a core liquidity provision venue. However, we will also add capabilities to deposit into a Uniswap v4 PYUSD/USDS pool as an additional option. We plan to use this pool for limit orders as part of our inventory rebalancing strategy (minimizing execution cost and the expense of speed), and also enabling us to place bids for USDS to meet ASC requirements and backstop the USDS peg.

Key parameters for Uniswap v4 integrations include the following: min lower tick and max upper tick limits are the lowest and highest tick where liquidity can be placed into the pool via deposit transactions, max tick width is the largest width of a single position, max slippage is the highest deviation from 1:1 price that we will accept for swap transactions, and maxAmount and slope parameters have the same meaning as for other SLL integrations. Note that min and max tick are scaled based on the difference in decimal precision of the included stablecoins; USDS uses 18 decimals while PYUSD and USDT use 6 decimals, meaning there is a scaling factor of 10E12 between them which is incorporated to determine the min and max tick. We also note the deviation from 1:1 price peg for simplicity and readability.

We consider the SLL relayer system to be untrusted, so we calculate the maximum potential loss over time if the relayer was compromised. For the Uniswap v4 integration, maximum losses are estimated as the volume of deposit and swap transactions that can be initiated until the system is recovered (eg via the freezer multisig blocking the compromised relayer), multiplied by the difference between market price and min/max tick limit or the max slippage respectively. With the below parameter configuration, we estimate a maximum potential loss from a compromised relayer of $90,000 assuming 12 hours time to recovery, which is within our risk tolerance and justified by the utility value of the integration.

Parameter summary:

* Uniswap v4 PYUSD/USDS Pool details
  * Pool: 0xe63e32b2ae40601662f760d6bf5d771057324fbd97784fe1d3717069f7b75d45
  * Swap fee: 0.0005%
* SLL parameters
  * Min lower tick limit: 276,314 (-0.1%)
  * Max upper tick limit: 276,334 (+0.1%)
  * Max tick width: 10 ticks
  * Max slippage: 0.1%
  * Deposits
    * maxAmount: 10 million
    * slope: 100 million per day
  * Withdrawals
    * maxAmount: 50 million
    * slope: 200 million per day
  * Swaps
    * maxAmount: 5 million
    * slope: 50 million per day

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard Uniswap v4 USDT/USDS Pool

Similar to above, we propose to onboard the USDT/USDS Uniswap v4 pool to enable limit orders and ASC management. Because USDT is more volatile than PYUSD or most other onshore stablecoins (on account of Tether’s mint and redeem fees), we may also be able to generate revenue through opportunistic limit orders and trading activity (stink bids, etc).

With the below parameter configuration, we estimate a maximum potential loss from a compromised relayer of $120,000 assuming 12 hours time to recovery, which is within our risk tolerance and justified by the utility value of the integration.

Parameter summary:

* Uniswap v4 USDT/USDS Pool details
  * Pool: 0x3b1b1f2e775a6db1664f8e7d59ad568605ea2406312c11aef03146c0cf89d5b9
  * Swap fee: 0.0005%
* SLL parameters
  * Min lower tick limit: 276,304 (-0.2%)
  * Max upper tick limit: 276,344 (+0.2%)
  * Max tick width: 10 ticks
  * Max slippage: 0.2%
  * Deposits
    * maxAmount: 5 million
    * slope: 50 million per day
  * Withdrawals
    * maxAmount: 50 million
    * slope: 200 million per day
  * Swaps
    * maxAmount: 5 million
    * slope: 50 million per day

### [Ethereum] Spark Savings - Increase spUSDT Supply Cap

We plan to grow the spUSDT savings vault significantly in the coming months, and propose to increase the deposit cap to allow for growth beyond the current 500 million USDT limit. We are planning to update the Spark Savings Configuration and Strategy section of the Spark Artifact to more closely align the spUSDT rewards rate with revenue generation, which should significantly reduce potential financial risk of higher spUSDT supply caps.

Parameter summary:

* spUSDT supply cap: 2 billion

### [Ethereum] SparkLend - Claim Accumulated Reserves and Send to Liquidation Multisig

SparkLend has accumulated significant reserves in various asset denominations. We propose to claim all reserves, which will allow them to be used to meet risk capital requirements (RRC). Now that Spark is capital constrained, we gain significant benefit from increasing our risk capital. Spark’s return on equity is equivalent to the spread of asset yield vs cost (Sky base rate), divided by capital risk rating (CRR) for the marginal asset that we would be forced to reduce allocation to in order to remain within RRC limits; in many cases this RoE may be 30-50% or more, indicating that consolidation of all assets into the subproxy as USDS outperforms any other possible investment strategy including holding the funds in the SparkLend reserves untouched.

For reserves of stablecoins in SparkLend that are supported by SLL, we can claim these directly to the ALM Proxy without a specific poll under standing authorization in the Spark artifact, and the increase in SLL NAV will be credited to the Spark subproxy as USDS profit as part of a future monthly settlement cycle. For non-USD assets or any USD assets that are not supported by SLL, we propose to transfer them to the Spark Operations Multisig to be liquidated for USDS, which will then be transferred back to the Spark subdao proxy.

We propose to add this as a recurring item for future spells, including this into the Spark Artifact at section A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.3 with the following language change (added text underlined):

A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.3 - Token Claim Authorization

Phoenix Labs is authorized to propose the inclusion of transfers of accrued treasury and collector revenues from the Active Instances to the Spark ALM Proxy in a Spark Spell. Additionally, for non-USD denominated reserve assets, or USD denominated reserve assets that are not supported by the Spark Liquidity Layer, the reserves can be transferred to the Spark Operations Multisig at address (0x2E1b01adABB8D4981863394bEa23a1263CBaeDfC) to be liquidated for USDS, with the proceeds of sale transferred to the Spark SubDAO Proxy. This request must be posted to the Sky Forum under the Spark Prime category. The Operational Executor Agent must formally approve the inclusion of the transfer in a Spark Spell, with no token holder vote needed.

Parameter summary:

* Claim SparkLend USDS, USDC, DAI, PYUSD, and USDT and send to SparkALM Proxy
  * Recipient address: 0x1601843c5E9bC251A3272907010AFa41Fa18347E
* Claim all other SparkLend reserves, and send to Spark Operations Multisig
  * Recipient address: 0x2E1b01adABB8D4981863394bEa23a1263CBaeDfC

**Edit (22 January 2026):** Updated the address for stablecoins to match the correct ALM proxy address 0x1601843c5E9bC251A3272907010AFa41Fa18347E.

### [Ethereum] Spark Treasury - Spark Foundation Grant for February 2026 (Exec)

Per the previously approved budget from the [December 11, 2025 spell](https://forum.skyeco.com/t/december-11-2025-proposed-changes-to-spark-for-upcoming-spell/27481), Spark will transfer 1,100,000 USDS to the Spark Foundation for February expenses as part of this spell. The transfer has already been approved by SPK governance and will proceed directly to the executive vote.

Parameter Summary: 

* Spark Foundation Grant
  * Recipient address: 0x92e4629a4510AF5819d7D1601464C233599fF5ec
  * Transfer amount: 1,100,000 USDS
